risks.description: Zinc-nickel plate is specified per PS-152.17A, but coating thickness, masking rules, and hydrogen-embrittlement bake requirements are not visible in the feature graph.
risks.mitigation: Run plating to PS-152.17A work instruction; confirm final/as-plated dimensional acceptance and any bake requirement before production release.

risks.description: The local 'BOND PREP THIS AREA' zone is called out by leader only and does not have exact axial or circumferential limits in the extracted feature graph.
risks.mitigation: Use customer/process standard PS-135.22 or buyer clarification to define the masked/prepared area; keep the operation as conditional for the quoted BS00B364350 item.

risks.description: The second lathe setup must hold a partly finished ball/stem without damaging the finished OD profile.
risks.mitigation: Use bored soft jaws or a protective form fixture made in the tool room; clamp on a robust Ø40/ball form with axial location from the left face.

risks.description: The Ø19 through passage requires drilling through 122.5 mm, which is greater than 6×D and needs chip-control attention.
risks.mitigation: Use peck drilling or through-coolant drilling, drill before final counterbore, and verify bore size with plug/bore gauges.

feasibility.assessment_notes: The component is a medium-carbon 1045 cold-drawn steel axisymmetric ball-stud. Its Ø63 × 122.5 mm envelope fits Almity CNC lathe and saw capacity. Explicit tolerances on Ø40, Ø19, Ø31.67, and 50 mm depth are within careful turning/boring capability. No heat treatment or grinding requirement is specified. Plating is routed in-house on the plating line, with specification-detail risk noted.

starting_dimensions.notes: Ø65 round bar gives approximately 1.0 mm radial stock over the Ø63 maximum ball diameter. 130 mm cut length gives 7.5 mm total axial allowance for facing to 122.5 mm final length.

stock.why: The finished geometry is fully rotational and the material is specified as 1045 cold-drawn steel, so round bar is the closest standard stock form. Although quantity is 1000, forging is not selected because the material condition is cold-drawn bar, no forging callout is present, and the shop profile does not list forging capability. CNC turning route and hole-making route are the dominant processes; plating follows after all machining and deburr.

evidence.cited_text: Overall part length 122.50 mm; maximum radial envelope Ø63.00; Ø40 external cylindrical stem.
Selected Ø65 × 130 mm round bar with turning/facing allowance.

route.route_name: Round-bar CNC turned ball-stud with axial bore/counterbore and zinc-nickel plating
route.route_reason: Overlay of turning, hole-making, and surface-treatment route families. The part is axisymmetric, all critical features are coaxial to the X axis, and tolerances are achievable by CNC turning/boring without heat treatment or grinding. Plating is sequenced after all machining and edge break.

setups.machine_reason: The shop saw is the correct first operation for cutting 1045 round bar to repeatable blank length before CNC turning.

workholding.method: bar clamped in CNC circular saw vise
workholding.grip_description: Clamp Ø65 round bar in saw vise with length stop set to 130.0 mm.

setups.stock_state_before: Commercial Ø65 SAE J403 Grade 1045 cold-drawn round bar.
setups.stock_state_after: Saw-cut Ø65 × 130 mm turning blank with extra length for facing.

sub_operations.operation_name: Saw cut Ø65 round bar to 130.0 mm blank length
sub_operations.reason: Prepares the round blank for lathe work with axial facing allowance before final 122.5 mm length is established.

tool.tool_description: CNC circular saw blade suitable for 1045 steel round bar
tool.notes: Use production length stop; do not hold final length at saw.

sub_operations.notes: Blank length is intentionally oversize; final length is established by lathe facing.

setups.inspection_at_setup: Verify blank length and bar diameter at first-off and periodically in batch.
setups.setup_reason: Stock preparation must precede all turning operations.

setups.setup_name: CNC lathe first side: left datum, ball/left OD, and small bore
setups.machine_reason: The features are coaxial about the X axis; CNC lathe is the lowest-cost and most accurate process for facing, OD contour turning, on-center drilling, and boring.

workholding.method: 3-jaw/soft-jaw chuck with live center support during OD roughing
workholding.grip_description: Grip on right-side stock allowance/rough OD. Face left end first, center support while roughing long OD contour, then remove center support for axial drilling and boring.
workholding.fixture_notes: Use soft jaws for repeatability; leave enough right-end stock for the second operation cleanup.

setups.stock_state_after: Left face established, left/ball OD contour roughed/finished where accessible, Ø18.5 through pilot drilled, Ø19 small bore finished to the counterbore-shoulder depth.

sub_operations.operation_name: Face left annular datum end
sub_operations.reason: Creates the axial reference face used for the 122.5 mm overall length and 33.5 mm ball-location dimension.

tool.tool_description: CNMG/WNMG style facing insert for 1045 steel

sub_operations.notes: This face becomes the reference for ball center offset and final length setup.

sub_operations.operation_name: Center drill on X axis for tailstock support and axial drilling start
sub_operations.reason: Center/spot drilling precedes the deep axial drill and provides support location during OD roughing.

tool.tool_description: 60-degree center drill or carbide spot drill
tool.notes: Spot only enough to start the deep drill and support center.

sub_operations.notes: Required by hole-making sequence before twist/deep drilling.

sub_operations.operation_name: Rough turn accessible OD profile from Ø65 stock toward Ø63 ball and Ø40 stem form
sub_operations.reason: Rough OD before internal boring to preserve rigidity and remove bulk material while tailstock support is available.

tool.tool_description: CNMG/WNMG roughing insert for steel
tool.notes: Use tailstock support due long overhang while roughing.

sub_operations.notes: Leaves finish stock for the OD profile and preserves grip stock for setup S003.

sub_operations.operation_name: Finish turn accessible Ø63 R31.5 ball contour and R2 blends
sub_operations.reason: Finishes the contoured ball/bulged region after roughing while the left datum and centerline are established.

tool.tool_description: DCMT/VBMT finishing insert or profiling insert with nose-radius compensation
tool.notes: Program R31.5 ball contour and R2 transition radii.

sub_operations.notes: Untoleranced ball radius/default tolerance should be confirmed from title block.

sub_operations.operation_name: Peck drill Ø18.5 pilot through 122.5 mm
sub_operations.reason: Creates the axial passage before finish boring and before the right-side counterbore removes the final 50 mm length.

tool.tool_description: Ø18.5 through-coolant or long-series drill for steel
tool.insert_grade: carbide or coated HSS for 1045 steel
tool.notes: Use peck cycle because depth is greater than 3×D.

sub_operations.notes: Final Ø19 tolerance is produced by the following finishing operation on the small-bore length.

sub_operations.operation_name: Finish bore small Ø19.00 passage to shoulder depth 72.5 mm
sub_operations.reason: Sizes the remaining small bore before the right-side counterbore is machined; the counterbore later removes the right 50 mm portion.

tool.tool_type: small boring bar or machine reamer
tool.tool_description: Ø19 finishing tool for axial bore
tool.notes: Tolerance is Ø19.15/18.85, so boring/reaming is sufficient; honing is not required.

sub_operations.notes: Finished small-bore length is taken as 122.5 - 50.0 = 72.5 mm after counterbore.

setups.inspection_at_setup: Check left face cleanup, Ø19 bore plug/bore gauge, and ball profile first-off before releasing batch.
setups.setup_reason: Establish datum and main axisymmetric features while the blank has maximum rigidity and grip stock.

setups.setup_name: CNC lathe second side: final right end, shank, and counterbore
setups.machine_reason: The right shank, counterbore, bore shoulder, and end face are all coaxial lathe features. CNC lathe preserves concentricity between ID and OD features.

workholding.grip_description: Locate from the finished left datum face and grip protected finished OD/ball form or Ø40 region in bored soft jaws; expose the right end for facing, OD turning, and boring.
workholding.fixture_notes: Tool-room-made soft jaws or form fixture recommended to avoid marring the spherical/bulged region at 1000-piece volume.

setups.stock_state_before: Partially turned blank with left datum, ball/left OD contour, and Ø19 bore started/finished to shoulder depth; right end still has cleanup and grip stock.
setups.stock_state_after: All lathe machining complete: 122.5 mm length, Ø40 right shank, Ø31.67 × 50 counterbore, internal shoulder, and turned chamfers/edge starts.

sub_operations.operation_name: Face right annular end to final 122.5 mm overall length
sub_operations.reason: Completes the opposite end face after flipping and establishes the final overall length from the left datum.

tool.tool_description: Facing insert for 1045 steel

sub_operations.notes: Confirm final length against left face F002.

sub_operations.operation_name: Rough turn right shank from stock OD to Ø40.5 over 50 mm
sub_operations.reason: Removes bulk right-end stock while leaving finish allowance; rough OD precedes ID finish but final OD waits until after counterbore because the wall is thin.

tool.tool_description: Roughing insert for Ø40 shank material removal

sub_operations.notes: Leave finish stock before counterbore is finalized.

sub_operations.operation_name: Rough bore right counterbore from Ø19 to approximately Ø31.2 × 50 mm
sub_operations.reason: Roughs the counterbore and starts the internal shoulder while leaving stock for final bore size and shoulder location.

tool.tool_description: Rigid boring bar suitable for Ø31.67 counterbore
tool.notes: Control chips to avoid damaging the Ø19 bore.

sub_operations.notes: Counterbore is blind to a shoulder 50.0 mm from the right face.

sub_operations.operation_name: Finish bore Ø31.67 counterbore and square internal shoulder at 50.0 mm
sub_operations.reason: Achieves the counterbore diameter limit and 50.00 ±0.13 mm shoulder depth before final OD sizing on the thin-wall shank.

tool.tool_type: fine boring bar / internal facing tool
tool.tool_description: Fine boring bar with internal shoulder-facing capability
tool.notes: Measure with bore gauge and depth micrometer.

sub_operations.notes: This operation creates the annular internal shoulder F005.

sub_operations.operation_name: Finish turn right Ø40.00 shank over 50 mm and blend to ball
sub_operations.reason: Final OD is cut after the counterbore to minimize thin-wall distortion and hold Ø40.13/39.87 on the shank.

tool.tool_description: Finishing insert with suitable nose radius for Ø40 shank and R2 blend
tool.notes: Final OD pass follows final counterbore due 4.165 mm wall thickness.

sub_operations.notes: Sequence satisfies thin-wall rule: finish ID before finish OD on the counterbored shank.

sub_operations.operation_name: Turn 0.5 mm max chamfers/edge breaks on accessible OD and bore mouths
sub_operations.reason: Starts the required edge break on lathe-accessible edges and prepares for plating adhesion.

tool.tool_description: Lathe chamfering tool for OD and ID edges
tool.notes: Final hand deburr follows before plating.

sub_operations.notes: Drawing limits edge break to 0.5 max; do not exceed this value.

setups.inspection_at_setup: Inspect overall length 122.5, Ø40 shank, Ø31.67 counterbore, 50.0 mm counterbore depth, and shoulder quality before deburr.
setups.setup_reason: Completes the second-side coaxial features while maintaining ID/OD relationship and final length.

setups.setup_name: Manual deburr, edge break, local bond-prep, and pre-plate cleaning
setups.machine_reason: Manual finish bench is appropriate for edge-break verification, localized surface preparation, cleaning, and masking/plug handling before plating.

workholding.grip_description: Support the turned part on V-blocks or soft pads to avoid marking the Ø63 ball and Ø40 shank.
workholding.fixture_notes: Use protective pads; avoid abrasive contact on surfaces not intended for bond prep.

setups.stock_state_before: All CNC lathe machining complete, with turned edge starts and possible machining burrs.
setups.stock_state_after: All sharp edges broken to 0.5 max, part cleaned/degreased for plating, local bond-prep performed if applicable.

sub_operations.operation_name: Hand deburr and verify all sharp edges 0.5 mm max
sub_operations.reason: Surface-treatment rules require sharp edges to be broken before coating; drawing explicitly calls out 0.5 max edge break.

tool.tool_description: Deburring blade, countersink hand tool, abrasive cloth, bore deburr brush
tool.notes: Do not exceed 0.5 mm edge break.

sub_operations.notes: Includes internal bore/counterbore mouth burrs and shoulder burr inspection.

sub_operations.operation_name: Conditional local bond-prep on indicated ball/stem zone
sub_operations.reason: Covers the drawing note 'BOND PREP THIS AREA' while flagging the missing zone boundary and variant/process ambiguity.

tool.tool_description: Approved solvent wipe and/or customer-approved abrasive media for bond-prep zone
tool.notes: Exact media and coverage must follow PS-135.22 or customer clarification.

sub_operations.notes: If RFQ is strictly BS00B364350 and PS-135.22 does not apply, hold this as a conditional operation.

sub_operations.operation_name: Pre-plate clean/degrease and plug or mask bores if required by PS-152.17A
sub_operations.reason: Surface-treatment route requires clean, oil-free surfaces before plating and masking/plugging wherever the plating specification or fit requires.

tool.tool_description: Solvent/alkaline clean materials and silicone or equivalent plugs if masking is required
tool.notes: Masking is specification-dependent because the drawing appears to call for general plating.

sub_operations.notes: Water-break cleanliness should pass before plating.

setups.inspection_at_setup: Visual check for burr-free edges, no sharp edges above 0.5 max, and correct local bond-prep/masking instructions before release to plating.
setups.setup_reason: Deburr and cleaning must occur after all machining and before plating for coating adhesion.

setups.machine_reason: The shop profile lists in-house plating capability; drawing calls for zinc-nickel plate, so surface treatment is routed after machining and deburr.

workholding.grip_description: Rack parts to maintain solution flow through the bore/counterbore and avoid contact marks on significant external surfaces.
workholding.fixture_notes: Use plating racks/contacts compatible with PS-152.17A and the required significant surfaces.

setups.stock_state_before: Clean, deburred machined steel part ready for coating.
setups.stock_state_after: Zinc-nickel plated part with post-treatment/passivation/bake per PS-152.17A.

sub_operations.operation_name: Apply zinc-nickel plate per PS-152.17A
sub_operations.reason: Satisfies the drawing general note requiring zinc-nickel plating after all machining is complete.

tool.tool_description: Rack plating arrangement for zinc-nickel plating
tool.notes: Confirm chemistry and thickness class to PS-152.17A.

sub_operations.notes: All machining is complete before plating; account for plating buildup per PS-152.17A.

sub_operations.operation_name: Post-plate passivation/seal and hydrogen-relief bake if required by PS-152.17A
sub_operations.reason: Surface-treatment route requires post-treatment/cure/bake as applicable. Material yield is below the common >1000 MPa bake trigger, but the customer plating specification may still require baking.

tool.tool_description: Passivation/seal/bake equipment per plating specification
tool.notes: Bake is conditional on PS-152.17A or customer strength threshold.

sub_operations.notes: No separate heat-treatment route is used; this is plating post-treatment only.

setups.inspection_at_setup: Verify plating bath record and required post-treatment record per PS-152.17A.
setups.setup_reason: Coating must be applied only after all machining and edge break are complete.

setups.machine_reason: Finish bench is appropriate for manual removal of any masking/plugs and final clean before inspection.

workholding.grip_description: Handle plated parts with clean gloves on soft pads to avoid coating damage.

setups.stock_state_before: Plated part with possible masking/plugs or post-treatment residue.
setups.stock_state_after: Clean plated part ready for dimensional and coating inspection.

sub_operations.operation_name: Remove any masks/plugs and clean plated part
sub_operations.reason: Removes process aids after plating and prepares the part for final inspection without damaging coating.

tool.tool_description: Clean gloves, soft pads, approved wipe/cleaner

sub_operations.notes: If no masking is required by PS-152.17A, this becomes final cleaning/handling only.

setups.inspection_at_setup: Visual check for complete mask removal and no coating damage.
setups.setup_reason: Final cleaning and unmasking occur after plating and before final inspection.

setups.machine_reason: The in-house inspection room is suitable for the specified general machining tolerances, bore gauges, micrometers, depth measurements, and coating visual/thickness checks.

workholding.grip_description: Support part on soft V-blocks or centers as needed for OD/ID and length checks.
workholding.fixture_notes: Use bore gauge/plug gauge for Ø19 and Ø31.67; use micrometers for Ø40 and Ø63 profile checks.

setups.stock_state_after: Accepted inspected component ready for marking/packaging per downstream requirements.

sub_operations.operation_name: Final dimensional inspection of turned geometry and bores
sub_operations.reason: Verifies all critical dimensions after machining and plating, including OD, ID, counterbore depth, shoulder, overall length, radii, and edge break.

tool.tool_description: OD micrometers, bore gauges/plug gauges, depth micrometer, radius/profile templates as required
tool.notes: CMM is not required unless customer FAI demands it.

sub_operations.notes: Inspect as-plated unless PS-152.17A defines pre-plate dimensional checks separately.

sub_operations.operation_name: Inspect zinc-nickel coating and local bond-prep zone
sub_operations.reason: Verifies finish note compliance, coating coverage/appearance/thickness per PS-152.17A, and bond-prep execution if applicable.

tool.tool_description: Visual inspection tools and coating thickness gauge/XRF if required by PS-152.17A
tool.notes: Salt spray or special testing only if required by PS-152.17A/customer PO.

sub_operations.notes: Bond-prep acceptance depends on clarification of zone limits and applicable part number.

setups.inspection_at_setup: Final acceptance inspection and coating records.
setups.setup_reason: Inspection is last after deburr, cleaning, coating, and unmasking.

routing_notes: Route families used: turning, hole_making, and surface_treatment. No organizational corrections were returned.
No heat treatment is specified; therefore no hard turning, grinding, or honing route is inserted.
For the thin counterbored right shank, rough OD first for rigidity, then finish the ID counterbore, then finish the Ø40 OD.
All machining and manual edge break must be complete before zinc-nickel plating.
The Ø19 through drilling is greater than 3×D, so peck drilling or through-coolant drilling is specified.
Plating thickness/masking must follow PS-152.17A; adjust pre-plate dimensions only if the coating specification requires final dimensions after plating.
Bond-prep operation is included as conditional because the parts table/process-note applicability is ambiguous for BS00B364350 versus BS00B364351.

clarifications.question: Confirm whether the RFQ is for BS00B364350 only or also includes the BS00B364351 configuration/process row.
clarifications.why_it_matters: The parts table lists BS00B364350 as 'AS SHOWN' and BS00B364351 as 'PS-135.22 *'. The starred bond-prep process may apply only to the 351 variant, which affects required surface processing.
clarifications.suggested_default: Treat RFQ item BS00B364350 as the as-shown geometry with zinc-nickel plating, and keep PS-135.22/bond-prep as conditional unless purchasing specifies BS00B364351.

clarifications.question: What are the exact axial and circumferential boundaries of the 'BOND PREP THIS AREA *' zone?
clarifications.why_it_matters: The leader identifies a local surface-treatment area but no numeric boundary dimensions are visible; exact coverage affects inspection, masking, and preparation.
clarifications.suggested_default: Use the leader-indicated external spherical/transition surface only; do not assume full 360-degree coverage unless PS-135.22 defines it.

clarifications.question: Confirm the title-block general tolerance values for untoleranced metric dimensions.
clarifications.why_it_matters: Explicit limit dimensions are clear, but untoleranced dimensions such as Ø63.00, R31.50, R2.0, 33.50, and 122.50 depend on the default tolerance standard.
clarifications.suggested_default: Use explicit limit dimensions where present; for untoleranced dimensions use the best-read title-block defaults: approximately X.X ±0.5, X.XX ±0.25, angles ±2 degrees.

clarifications.question: What coating thickness class, masking requirements, and hydrogen-relief bake requirements apply under PS-152.17A?
clarifications.why_it_matters: Zinc-nickel plating changes OD and ID dimensions; masking or pre-plate dimensional offsets may be needed to meet final fit. Bake requirements depend on the plating spec and steel strength/hardness criteria.
clarifications.suggested_default: Plate all significant surfaces per PS-152.17A, verify dimensions as-plated, and bake only if PS-152.17A/customer requirements trigger it for this 1045 CD material.

assumptions.text: Production quantity is taken as 1000 pieces per the user-provided production context, because the feature graph quantity is null.

The part is routed from 1045 cold-drawn round bar rather than forging because the drawing material callout includes CD/cold-drawn, no forging requirement is shown, and the shop profile does not list forging capability.
No heat treatment is required because the feature graph has heat_treatment=null and no hardness/case-depth callout is present.
Starting blank is selected as Ø65 × 130 mm round bar, providing approximately 1.0 mm radial allowance over Ø63 and 7.5 mm total facing allowance over 122.5 mm final length.
Local bond-prep is retained as a conditional finish-bench step until the buyer confirms whether the PS-135.22 starred process applies to BS00B364350.
Zinc-nickel plating can be handled on the in-house plating line, subject to PS-152.17A chemistry/thickness confirmation; no outside process is planned by default.

analysis_trace.summary: The part is an axisymmetric 1045 cold-drawn steel ball-stud, 122.5 mm long, with Ø63/R31.5 bulged ball region, Ø40 shank, Ø19 axial bore, Ø31.67 × 50 right counterbore, internal shoulder, 0.5 max edge break, local bond-prep note, and zinc-nickel plating.
issues: No formal datums; left end face is used as the practical axial reference.
Bond-prep boundary and process applicability are unclear.
Plating thickness/masking details are not visible.

analysis_trace.summary: Retrieved route sequences for turning, hole_making, and surface_treatment. Turning drives the OD/ID/facing route; hole-making adds center/peck/boring rules for the deep axial hole; surface treatment anchors plating after all machining and deburr.
issues: Forging was considered because of 1000-piece automotive context, but not selected due cold-drawn material callout and no shop forging capability.
No heat-treatment, grinding, gear, milling, broaching, or sheet-metal route applies.

analysis_trace.summary: Classified as part_family='turned' and base_geometry='rotational' because nearly all features are coaxial lathe-generated surfaces.

Searched routing corrections for a turned rotational 1045/mild-steel component with external cylinder, internal bore, counterbore, spherical boss, edge break, zinc-nickel plating, and bond-prep zone at 1000-piece volume. No corrections were returned.
Selected Ø65 × 130 mm SAE J403 Grade 1045 cold-drawn round bar. Round bar best matches the rotational geometry and material condition, and it can be processed in-house by saw and CNC lathe.
issues: Stock allowance is a planning selection and should be confirmed against available standard bar sizes and plating/pre-finish policy.

analysis_trace.summary: Built route as saw cut → lathe first side → lathe second side → manual deburr/bond prep/clean → plating → post-plate clean → final inspection. Key sequencing rules are satisfied: rough before finish, OD before ID where possible, center drill before deep drill, finish ID before final OD on the thin counterbored shank, and all machining/edge break before plating.
issues: Second lathe operation needs soft jaws/form holding to protect the finished ball/stem.
Bond-prep and plating spec details remain clarification items.

analysis_trace.summary: Expanded each setup into atomic sub-operations with valid machine_family and operation_type values. Formula inputs use feature graph geometry where available; process-selected values such as stock size, rough allowance, and number of rough passes are identified as agent estimates.
issues: Exact bond-prep zone area cannot be numerically populated; external diameter and part length are provided while clarification is requested.

analysis_trace.summary: Feature coverage checked: F002 through F011 are covered by machining, deburr, plating, bond-prep, or inspection operations. Stock states progress logically through saw, turning, finishing, plating, and inspection. Machine selections are all valid keys from the shop profile.
issues: Coating thickness/masking can affect final dimensions and must be confirmed before production release.
